title: amaryllis × pallidiflora
intro: Amaryllis × pallidiflora, a hybrid of the amaryllis plant, is a flowering bulbous perennial native to South Africa, characterized by its unique pale yellow or white trumpet-shaped blooms. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Amaryllis × pallidiflora include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
